title: Team Lunch Ordering
betHeadline: One coordinated team lunch order beats ten individual ones.
jtbd: When a team wants to eat together at the office, help them order in one coordinated flow so no one is left out and it all arrives together.
seeking: Evidence that coordination friction is the real blocker, not price or selection.

confidence.label: Medium
confidence.reason: Strong behavioral signal, but the retention impact is still modeled, not measured.

executiveSummary.text: 38% of office orders happen within a 30-minute lunch window
Group orders carry a 2.3x higher average basket than solo orders
Coordination, not price, is the top-cited friction in team lunch ordering

dimensions.label: Size
dimensions.note: 1 of 2 sizing inputs live — attach rate still modeled.

dimensions.label: Evidence quality
dimensions.note: 1 of 3 claims still assumed, not measured.

gap.body: Feasibility — the build/ops path is unproven. A scoped prototype or ops dry-run would show whether it can ship.

conviction.verdictLine: Feasibility is holding this back — close it to decide.
